events.label: assistant
events.text: Written to `/app/answer.json`. The core issue: OSHA 1910.147(c)(5) requires each isolation point to have both a lockout device and a tagout device identifying the authorized employee — a lock without a tag (or vice versa) breaks the isolate-and-verify sequence, since the point can't be confirmed as complete or attributable. Marked `fail` / `high` risk, with corrective actions to apply the missing device and re-verify zero-energy state before work continues.
